_doc: IMMUTABLE PRESENT-LENS BASELINE IDENTITY for the v2.11 lineage (owner/supervisor rulings 2026-07-20). balanced_board_md5 identifies the accepted PRESENT-LENS baseline — the accepted present `v` values and present rankings represented by 06d8af60. It is a fixed lineage ANCHOR that is CONSTANT across weekly rounds: a weekly score transaction updates the CURRENT board + store + as_of_round + the per-report board/store before-and-after chain, but NEVER these baseline fields. The fixed MODEL identity pins (engine_head / rl_model / fv / config / register) are read from data/expected_boot.json (the governing boot manifest, which the weekly updater never re-stamps for the model); only the baseline fields (release_version + balanced_board_md5) not present in the boot manifest live here.
_present_lens_only: IMPORTANT (addendum 2026-07-20): balanced_board_md5 = 06d8af60 is the accepted PRESENT-LENS baseline identity ONLY. It is NOT assumed to be the final COMPLETE board-file artifact hash. The owner has accepted the present `v` values + rankings but has NOT accepted the old vP1/vP2 forward-lens outputs; once Leg E/F forward-lens fields are adopted, the complete board artifact (and its full-board hash) will differ. The moving `board` field in a report is the complete CURRENT board artifact; balanced_board_md5 anchors the present-lens lineage only.
_not_final_rc: This file does NOT claim final RC compatibility and does NOT represent the final release policy. The candidate final release-policy switches (RL_PVC2 / RL_LEGE / RL_LEGF) are UNRESOLVED and subject to a separate read-only forward-lens acceptance + owner viewing; this file encodes NO switch posture (it does not assume 1/0/0, 1/1/1, or any final value). release_version below is the present-lens-baseline lineage label, not a claim that the current manifest is the final release.
_external_integration_dependencies: canonical switch-manifest closure (data/model_config.json does not yet express all release-semantic switches)
final Leg E/F forward-lens owner acceptance
final full-board regeneration and pinning (the final complete board-file hash is not yet known)
club-valuation regeneration (owned by the Track A release contract)
_model_pins_source: data/expected_boot.json (engine_head, rl_model, fv, config, register — fixed across weekly rounds; re-stamped only at a separately-approved valuation-changing release)
_rc_note: On RC integration (PR #127) this file is superseded by the RC manifest's verified release_version + balanced_board_md5 (and the final full-board pin once Leg E/F is accepted); the weekly updater copies those verified fields, it never synthesizes them.
_final_integration_2026_07_21: RESOLVED in integration/v2.11-final-rc: the canonical switch-manifest closure (data/model_config.json now stamps all class-A switches incl. RL_PVC2/RL_LEGE/RL_LEGF=1 + data/release_contract.json), the Leg E/F forward-lens owner acceptance (Board B), and the final full-board regeneration + pinning (board 039ff8d4; expected_boot 'board') are all DONE. balanced_board_md5 06d8af60 STAYS the immutable present-lens baseline anchor — the final board preserves the present v + rankings exactly (0 movers). release_version below remains the present-lens-baseline lineage label; the governing release version is data/expected_boot.json 'release_version' (v2.11-final-rc1-PROVISIONAL).

release_transition._doc: OWNER-APPROVED PROVENANCE TRANSITION (ITEM 408 Items 6-7, Option A; owner Luke McAlister via execution supervisor GPT Sol 5.6, 2026-07-23). The authorised Round 15-19 recovery is genuine production Movers history. These reports were generated under the earlier accepted PRESENT-LENS release (`source`); the application has since advanced to the current accepted release (`destination`). This record is the SEPARATELY-DECLARED, owner-approved bridge that lets the UI display the historical reports under the current release WITHOUT pretending they were generated under it, and WITHOUT rewriting any report. The browser validator (ui/app/movers.js core) requires this record — matching the historical reports EXACTLY on `source` and the loaded application EXACTLY on `destination`, with the reports' content digest intact — before it will display a historical bundle whose identity differs from the loaded release. Absent / partial / wrong / reversed / tampered => fail closed. It authorises NO score application (gate OFF), NO rewrite of history, and NO board-of-record / store / ledger / per-round-movers movement.
release_transition.owner_ruling: The authorised Round 15-19 recovery counts as genuine production Movers history. Retain the R15-R19 production Movers reports; do not reset the production Movers bundle to empty; preserve the historical reports and implement a fail-closed provenance bridge to the current accepted release identity. No historical report may be silently rewritten.
release_transition.authority: Luke McAlister (owner) — sole STOP-1/STOP-2/merge authority; relayed by GPT Sol 5.6; recorded by the claude-code-builder build seat (no interpretive authority).

applies_to.digest_algorithm: sha256 over canonical JSON (recursively key-sorted; JS JSON.stringify number semantics, e.g. 59.0->"59") of {str(round): report} for `rounds`. Computed identically by engine/rl_after/ingestion/round_movers.canonical_reports_digest (Python) and ui/app/movers.js core.reportsDigest (browser/node).
